d.t: Tìm hiểu sản phẩm qua đánh giá tích cực

e.p: I'm looking for a new coffee machine. Have you seen any good reviews for this brand?
e.m: Cho biết thông tin chung về thương hiệu máy pha cà phê đó từ các đánh giá.
e.h: Sử dụng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về thông tin chung.
e.s: Yes, I saw some reviews. People say this brand is very reliable, and their machines make delicious coffee.

e.p: Many people like this phone's camera. What did the reviews say about its photo quality?
e.m: Kể lại hai điểm tích cực về chất lượng ảnh của chiếc điện thoại.
e.h: Sử dụng cấu trúc 'It has...' hoặc 'People say...'.
e.s: Reviews mentioned the phone takes very clear pictures. Also, it has good colors, even in low light.

e.p: This new restaurant has great reviews. What kind of food do they serve there?
e.m: Nêu hai món ăn hoặc loại hình ẩm thực phổ biến được khách hàng khen ngợi.
e.h: Dùng 'They serve...' hoặc 'Customers like...'.
e.s: Customers love their Italian food, especially the pasta. Many reviews say the pizza is also very good.

e.p: I need a new pair of running shoes. This model has many positive reviews. What did people say about its comfort and durability?
e.m: Mô tả hai đặc điểm nổi bật (thoải mái, bền) của đôi giày theo đánh giá.
e.h: Sử dụng 'People often say...' và 'It is also...'.
e.s: People often say these shoes are super comfortable for long runs. They are also very durable, so they last a long time.

e.p: I'm thinking about buying this smart TV. Reviews are good. What features did customers like the most, and why?
e.m: Nêu hai tính năng khách hàng yêu thích và lý do tại sao họ thích chúng.
e.h: Dùng 'Customers liked...' và giải thích bằng 'because'.
e.s: Customers liked the picture quality because it is very clear. Also, they liked the easy-to-use smart features, so it's simple to find shows.

d.t: Xác định vấn đề qua đánh giá tiêu cực

e.p: I saw some bad reviews for this hotel. What was the main complaint?
e.m: Nêu một vấn đề chính mà khách hàng không hài lòng về khách sạn.
e.h: Sử dụng 'Many people said...' hoặc 'The main problem was...'.
e.s: Many people said the rooms were not very clean. Some reviews also mentioned noise at night.

e.p: This vacuum cleaner looks cheap, but some reviews say it has problems. What did they mention?
e.m: Kể hai vấn đề thường gặp của máy hút bụi này theo các đánh giá tiêu cực.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại.
e.s: Reviews mentioned the suction power was weak. Also, some customers said it broke easily after a few months.

e.p: I read a review about this app, and someone was very disappointed. What went wrong with the app?
e.m: Nêu hai lý do khiến người dùng thất vọng với ứng dụng đó.
e.h: Sử dụng 'Users were disappointed because...' hoặc 'It had issues with...'.
e.s: Users were disappointed because the app often crashed. Also, it had issues with ads, so many people found them annoying.

e.p: This restaurant's service got some bad reviews. What specifically made customers unhappy?
e.m: Mô tả hai khía cạnh cụ thể về dịch vụ khiến khách hàng không vui và cảm xúc của họ.
e.h: Dùng 'Customers complained about...' và 'They felt...'.
e.s: Customers complained about the slow service. They also felt the staff was not friendly, so many people were unhappy.

e.p: I was going to order this jacket online, but a review said it was not like the picture. What was the problem, and why was the customer upset?
e.m: Mô tả vấn đề về sản phẩm (không giống hình) và lý do khách hàng tức giận.
e.h: Sử dụng 'The problem was...' và 'The customer was angry because...'.
e.s: The problem was the jacket's color was different from the picture. The customer was angry because the material was also very cheap, not good quality.

d.t: Đưa ra quyết định mua hàng

e.p: This new blender has good reviews and a good price. Do you think I should buy it?
e.m: Cho lời khuyên ngắn gọn về việc mua sản phẩm này.
e.h: Sử dụng 'Yes, you should...' hoặc 'I think you should...'.
e.s: Yes, you should buy it. It sounds like a great product for the price.

e.p: I want a new camera, but this one has mixed reviews – some good, some bad. What should I do?
e.m: Khuyên nên tìm hiểu thêm hoặc cân nhắc lựa chọn khác.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should...' hoặc 'Maybe you should...'.
e.s: You should read more reviews from different websites. Maybe you should also look for another camera model.

e.p: This hotel is expensive, but it has excellent reviews for its service and location. Should I book it for my trip?
e.m: Cho lời khuyên nên đặt phòng khách sạn, nêu hai lý do chính.
e.h: Dùng 'I think you should...' và giải thích 'because'.
e.s: I think you should book it. It sounds expensive, but the service is excellent and the location is very good, so you will enjoy your trip.

e.p: I need a new laptop for work, and this one is on sale. The reviews are mostly positive, but some say the battery life is short. Should I still get it?
e.m: Đưa ra lời khuyên cân nhắc điểm mạnh và điểm yếu, và có thể tìm kiếm thêm.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should think about...' và 'Perhaps you could...'.
e.s: You should think about how important battery life is for your work. Perhaps you could find another laptop with better battery life, even if it's not on sale.

e.p: My friend wants to buy a drone. This one is popular and has great reviews, especially for its camera. But it's quite expensive. What would you tell her?
e.m: Khuyên bạn nên cân nhắc giữa chất lượng và giá cả, và quyết định dựa trên nhu cầu của mình.
e.h: Sử dụng 'She should consider...' và 'If she wants...'.
e.s: She should consider if the camera quality is worth the high price for her. If she wants the best pictures, then it might be a good choice, but if not, she can look for a cheaper one.

d.t: Chia sẻ trải nghiệm mua sắm và đánh giá

e.p: Did you buy that new speaker? What do you think about it after using it for a while?
e.m: Chia sẻ cảm nhận của bạn về chất lượng âm thanh của loa.
e.h: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để diễn tả cảm nhận.
e.s: Yes, I bought it last week. The sound quality is amazing, and it is very easy to connect.

e.p: Last month, you bought a new travel bag. Was it good for your trip? Tell me about it.
e.m: Kể về trải nghiệm dùng chiếc túi, tập trung vào hai điểm bạn thích.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại sự kiện.
e.s: Yes, it was very good. It was light and had many pockets, so I could pack a lot of things.

e.p: You visited that new café everyone is talking about. What was your experience like? Did you like the coffee?
e.m: Mô tả trải nghiệm của bạn ở quán cà phê, kể về không gian và đồ uống.
e.h: Sử dụng thì quá khứ đơn và cấu trúc 'I liked... because...'.
e.s: The café was really nice. I liked the cozy atmosphere, and the coffee was delicious because it was fresh.

e.p: I saw you bought some smart home devices. Were they easy to set up, and are you happy with how they work?
e.m: Kể lại việc cài đặt dễ hay khó, và cảm nhận chung của bạn về hiệu suất của thiết bị.
e.h: Sử dụng thì quá khứ đơn để mô tả hành động và thì hiện tại để nói cảm nhận.
e.s: They were quite easy to set up, but it took some time. I am happy with how they work, so my home is smarter now.

e.p: You often order food online from different restaurants. Which service do you like best, and why? Do you usually read reviews before ordering?
e.m: Nêu dịch vụ bạn thích nhất và hai lý do. Cho biết bạn có đọc đánh giá trước khi đặt món không.
e.h: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói thói quen và 'because' để giải thích.
e.s: I like 'FoodGo' best because their delivery is fast, and they have many choices. Yes, I always read reviews before ordering because I want good food.

d.t: So sánh sản phẩm dựa trên đánh giá

e.p: I'm choosing between two smartwatches. This one is cheaper, but the other has better reviews. What should I consider?
e.m: Khuyên nên xem xét chất lượng so với giá cả.
e.h: Dùng cấu trúc so sánh hơn.
e.s: You should consider if the cheaper one is good enough. Often, better reviews mean better quality, even if it's more expensive.

e.p: Many reviews say this new tablet has a faster processor, but the old model has a longer battery life. Which one sounds better for gaming?
e.m: So sánh hai chiếc máy tính bảng và đưa ra lựa chọn phù hợp cho việc chơi game.
e.h: Sử dụng 'X is better than Y' và 'because'.
e.s: The new tablet is better for gaming because a faster processor makes games run smoother. Battery life is important, but speed is more critical for gaming.

e.p: I'm comparing two vacuum cleaners. Model A is more expensive but reviews say it's very quiet. Model B is cheaper but some reviews mention it's loud. Which one would you choose for a quiet home?
e.m: So sánh hai mẫu máy hút bụi và khuyên chọn mẫu phù hợp cho một ngôi nhà yên tĩnh.
e.h: Dùng cấu trúc so sánh hơn và 'so' để kết nối.
e.s: For a quiet home, Model A is better, even if it's more expensive. A loud vacuum cleaner can be very annoying, so Model B might not be good.

e.p: I need a new laptop for school. This one has great reviews for its light weight, but the other one is heavier but has better performance for drawing. Which one should I get?
e.m: So sánh hai máy tính xách tay và khuyên chọn dựa trên nhu cầu học tập và vẽ.
e.h: Sử dụng 'If you need...' và cấu trúc so sánh hơn.
e.s: If you need it for drawing, the heavier laptop is better because it has better performance. A light laptop is nice, but drawing needs a powerful computer, so it is more important.

e.p: There are two popular hotels in the city. Hotel A has slightly worse reviews for food but is closer to the beach. Hotel B has excellent food reviews but is far from the beach. Which one would be better for a family vacation with kids?
e.m: So sánh hai khách sạn và khuyên chọn cho kỳ nghỉ gia đình có trẻ nhỏ, nêu rõ hai lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'For a family with kids...' và giải thích 'because'.
e.s: For a family with kids, Hotel A is better because being closer to the beach is more fun for them. Also, kids might not care so much about excellent food, so the location is more important.

d.t: Đánh giá dịch vụ khách hàng

e.p: I had a problem with my new phone, and the store's customer service was very good. How was your experience with their service?
e.m: Kể về một trải nghiệm tích cực với dịch vụ khách hàng của bạn.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để mô tả sự kiện.
e.s: My experience was also good. They were very helpful, and they fixed my issue quickly.

e.p: I read some reviews that said this online shop has very slow customer service. Do you think I should still buy from them?
e.m: Khuyên không nên mua hàng và giải thích lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'I don't think you should...' và 'because'.
e.s: I don't think you should buy from them. Slow customer service is a big problem, because if you have issues, they might not help you well.

e.p: I called my internet provider yesterday, and the person on the phone was quite rude. How would you rate their customer service based on your experiences?
e.m: Đánh giá dịch vụ khách hàng của nhà cung cấp internet đó dựa trên kinh nghiệm của bạn, nêu hai điểm.
e.h: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về ý kiến và thì quá khứ đơn để kể trải nghiệm.
e.s: I think their customer service is not very good. Last month, I waited a long time to talk to someone, and the person was also not very helpful.

e.p: This travel agency has excellent reviews for customer support. They help quickly if there are problems. What kind of questions should I ask them before booking my trip?
e.m: Đề xuất hai câu hỏi cụ thể để hỏi công ty du lịch về việc hỗ trợ khách hàng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should ask...' để đưa ra gợi ý.
e.s: You should ask about their cancellation policy if plans change. Also, ask if they have 24/7 support in case of emergencies, so you are always safe.

e.p: A friend told me she had a bad experience with the customer service of a popular airline. She was very disappointed. What do you think the airline should do to improve their service, based on common complaints?
e.m: Đề xuất hai cách để hãng hàng không cải thiện dịch vụ khách hàng, dựa trên các vấn đề phổ biến.
e.h: Sử dụng 'They should...' hoặc 'They need to...' để đưa ra đề xuất.
e.s: They should train their staff to be more friendly and helpful to passengers. Also, they need to respond faster to customer complaints, because slow responses make people very angry.

d.t: Tìm kiếm sản phẩm giá tốt qua đánh giá

e.p: I'm looking for a cheap laptop that still has good reviews. Do you know any good models?
e.m: Đề xuất một mẫu laptop giá rẻ mà vẫn được đánh giá tốt.
e.h: Dùng 'I heard that...' hoặc 'Many people like...'.
e.s: I heard that the 'TechPro Basic' laptop is good. It's not expensive, and many people like its performance for daily tasks.

e.p: This vacuum cleaner is on sale, but it doesn't have many reviews. Should I still consider buying it?
e.m: Khuyên nên cẩn thận và tìm kiếm thêm thông tin trước khi mua.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should be careful...' và 'because'.
e.s: You should be careful. A sale is good, but without many reviews, it's hard to know the quality. Maybe you should find more information first.

e.p: I want to buy a new pair of headphones, but I don't want to spend too much. Which brands have good quality products with reasonable prices, according to reviews?
e.m: Đề xuất hai thương hiệu tai nghe có giá cả hợp lý và chất lượng tốt theo đánh giá.
e.h: Dùng 'People often say...' và 'They offer...'.
e.s: People often say 'SoundMax' and 'AudioLine' offer good value. They have good sound quality, and their prices are usually cheaper than other big brands.

e.p: I found a great discount on this smartphone, but some recent reviews say its battery life got worse after an update. Should I buy it now or wait?
e.m: Khuyên nên đợi hoặc tìm kiếm thêm thông tin về vấn đề pin trước khi quyết định mua.
e.h: Dùng 'You should wait...' hoặc 'It's better to...'.
e.s: You should wait and see if the company fixes the battery problem. It's better to buy a phone with good battery life, even if the discount is good now.

e.p: My sister wants a new gaming console, but her budget is limited. What are some older models that still have excellent game libraries and strong positive reviews, making them a smart choice for a cheaper price?
e.m: Đề xuất hai mẫu máy chơi game cũ hơn mà vẫn được đánh giá cao về thư viện game và giá cả, giải thích lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'She should look at...' và 'because'.
e.s: She should look at the 'GameStation 4' or 'X-Play One'. They are older but still have many great games, and they are much cheaper now because new models came out.

d.t: Phân tích ảnh hưởng của đánh giá đến giá cả

e.p: This new product is getting amazing reviews, and its price is going up. Do you think good reviews always make prices rise?
e.m: Cho biết ý kiến của bạn về mối liên hệ giữa đánh giá tốt và giá cả.
e.h: Sử dụng 'I think so...' hoặc 'Usually, yes...'.
e.s: Usually, yes, I think so. When a product has good reviews, more people want it, so the company can raise the price.

e.p: I noticed that products with bad reviews often go on sale. Why does that happen?
e.m: Giải thích tại sao sản phẩm bị đánh giá xấu thường được giảm giá.
e.h: Dùng 'It happens because...' và 'so'.
e.s: It happens because stores want to sell them. If a product has bad reviews, fewer people want to buy it at the normal price, so they put it on sale.

e.p: This new restaurant was very popular last month, but now its reviews are getting worse, and prices have dropped. What do you think happened?
e.m: Giải thích lý do có thể khiến đánh giá xấu đi và giá giảm.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để đưa ra giả thuyết và 'so'.
e.s: Maybe the food quality got worse, or the service became slower. Bad reviews mean fewer customers, so the restaurant had to drop prices.

e.p: A lot of people bought this new smartphone because of its strong positive reviews, even though it was expensive. Now, a newer model is coming out. What do you expect will happen to the price of the old model?
e.m: Dự đoán điều gì sẽ xảy ra với giá của mẫu điện thoại cũ khi mẫu mới ra mắt, giải thích hai lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'I expect...' và 'because'.
e.s: I expect the price of the old model will go down a lot. This is because people will want the new model, and the store will want to sell the old ones faster.

e.p: Some luxury brands rarely have big sales, even if a particular product gets mixed reviews. Why do you think their prices remain high despite not-so-perfect feedback?
e.m: Giải thích lý do thương hiệu xa xỉ giữ giá cao bất chấp đánh giá, nêu hai yếu tố.
e.h: Dùng 'They keep prices high because...' và 'Also, they want...'.
e.s: They keep prices high because their brand image is about luxury and exclusivity. Also, they want people to feel that their products are special, so they don't lower prices often.

d.t: Xử lý một đánh giá tiêu cực

e.p: I just saw a very bad review for my favorite café. Someone said the coffee was terrible. What should I do?
e.m: Khuyên nên tự mình kiểm tra lại chất lượng cà phê.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should...' hoặc 'It's a good idea to...'.
e.s: You should go there again and try the coffee yourself. Maybe that customer had a bad day, or their taste is different.

e.p: My online store just received a 1-star review saying the delivery was very slow. What should I tell the customer?
e.m: Nói rằng bạn xin lỗi và sẽ kiểm tra vấn đề giao hàng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'I am sorry...' và 'I will...'.
e.s: I am sorry that your delivery was slow. I will check what happened with your order right away.

e.p: I bought a new lamp, but a customer review says it stopped working after one week. I feel a bit worried. What would you do if you were me?
e.m: Khuyên nên giữ hóa đơn và liên hệ cửa hàng nếu sản phẩm có vấn đề.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should keep...' và 'If it stops...'.
e.s: You should keep your receipt safe. If it stops working, you can contact the store, and they should help you get a new one or your money back.

e.p: A review for my travel agency said our guided tour was boring and the guide was unfriendly. How can I respond to this review professionally, asking for more details?
e.m: Viết phản hồi xin lỗi và hỏi thêm chi tiết để cải thiện dịch vụ.
e.h: Sử dụng 'We are sorry...' và 'Could you tell us...'.
e.s: We are very sorry to hear you had a bad experience. Could you tell us more details about your tour so we can improve our service?

e.p: I read a review where a customer was very angry because the product arrived broken. The company did not offer a refund. What should the company have done differently, and what should they do now to fix this?
e.m: Đề xuất hai hành động công ty nên làm khác đi và hai hành động nên làm ngay bây giờ để giải quyết vấn đề.
e.h: Sử dụng 'They should have...' và 'Now, they must...'.
e.s: They should have offered a refund or a new product immediately. Now, they must contact the customer and give them a full refund, and also say sorry for the bad experience.

d.t: Viết một đánh giá của riêng bạn

e.p: You just finished a book. What do you think about it? Can you write a very short review?
e.m: Nêu cảm nhận chung của bạn về cuốn sách và một điểm bạn thích.
e.h: Dùng 'I really liked...' và 'The story was...'.
e.s: I really liked this book. The story was exciting, and I read it very fast.

e.p: You recently bought a new pair of headphones. Write a simple review about its sound quality and comfort.
e.m: Mô tả chất lượng âm thanh và độ thoải mái của tai nghe.
e.h: Dùng 'The sound is...' và 'They are also...'.
e.s: The sound is clear and powerful. They are also very comfortable to wear for a long time.

e.p: You just stayed at a hotel. Write a short review about the cleanliness of the room and the friendliness of the staff.
e.m: Mô tả hai yếu tố bạn thích hoặc không thích về khách sạn.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại trải nghiệm.
e.s: The room was very clean, and I liked that. The staff was also friendly and helpful, so I had a good stay.

e.p: You ordered food from a new restaurant online. Write a review, mentioning the taste of the food, the delivery speed, and whether you would order again.
e.m: Đánh giá về món ăn, tốc độ giao hàng và cho biết liệu bạn có đặt hàng lại không.
e.h: Dùng 'The food was...' và 'The delivery was...' và 'I will...'.
e.s: The food was delicious and fresh. The delivery was also very fast, so I will definitely order from them again soon.

e.p: You visited a new amusement park last weekend. Write a detailed review about your experience, including the rides, the crowd, and if it's worth the price. What was one thing you loved, and one thing you would improve?
e.m: Mô tả trải nghiệm tổng thể, nêu một điểm bạn thích và một điểm bạn muốn cải thiện.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại sự kiện và thì hiện tại đơn để đưa ra ý kiến.
e.s: The park was fun, and the rides were exciting, but it was too crowded. I loved the big roller coaster, but they should improve the waiting lines because they were very long.

d.t: Đề xuất sản phẩm cho người khác

e.p: My sister wants a new pair of headphones. She likes listening to music when she exercises. What should I recommend?
e.m: Đề xuất một loại tai nghe phù hợp cho việc tập thể dục.
e.h: Dùng 'You should recommend...' hoặc 'I think she should...'.
e.s: I think she should get wireless headphones. They are better for exercise because there are no cables.

e.p: My friend needs a new coffee maker that is easy to use and makes good coffee. Which one would you suggest based on reviews?
e.m: Đề xuất một loại máy pha cà phê, nêu hai ưu điểm.
e.h: Dùng 'I would suggest...' và 'because'.
e.s: I would suggest the 'EasyBrew 3000'. Reviews say it is easy to use and makes great coffee because it has simple buttons.

e.p: My cousin is looking for a new smartphone for his elderly mother. She needs something with a simple interface and a good battery. What should he consider?
e.m: Đề xuất một số đặc điểm quan trọng cần tìm ở điện thoại cho người lớn tuổi, nêu hai điểm.
e.h: Sử dụng 'He should look for...' và 'Also, it needs...'.
e.s: He should look for a phone with big icons and a simple menu. Also, it needs a long battery life because she might forget to charge it often.

e.p: My neighbor wants to buy a new car, but he's worried about gas prices. He needs a reliable car for daily commuting. What kind of car should he look for, according to economic car reviews?
e.m: Đề xuất một loại xe tiết kiệm nhiên liệu, nêu hai lý do liên quan đến hiệu suất và chi phí.
e.h: Sử dụng 'He should look for...' và 'because'.
e.s: He should look for a small, fuel-efficient car or a hybrid model. These cars use less gas, so they are cheaper to run every day, and many reviews say they are reliable.

e.p: My colleague is planning a family trip with two small kids, and she needs a hotel that is family-friendly and has good service. What specific features should I tell her to look for in hotel reviews to ensure a good experience for her family?
e.m: Đề xuất hai tính năng khách sạn cần tìm trong đánh giá để đảm bảo phù hợp với gia đình có trẻ nhỏ, và giải thích tại sao.
e.h: Dùng 'She should look for reviews mentioning...' và 'because'.
e.s: She should look for reviews mentioning a swimming pool or a playground for kids because they need entertainment. Also, check for hotels that offer family rooms, so there is enough space and comfort for everyone.

d.t: Đánh giá về trải nghiệm mua sắm trực tuyến

e.p: Do you often buy things online? What do you like most about it?
e.m: Nêu một ưu điểm bạn thích nhất khi mua sắm trực tuyến.
e.h: Sử dụng 'I like...' và 'because'.
e.s: Yes, I buy things online often. I like the convenience most, because I can shop from home.

e.p: Some people worry about online shopping. What is one thing you don't like about it, or one problem you have had?
e.m: Nêu một vấn đề bạn không thích hoặc đã gặp phải khi mua sắm trực tuyến.
e.h: Dùng 'I don't like...' hoặc 'Sometimes, I have...'.
e.s: I don't like when the delivery takes too long. Sometimes, I have to wait many days for my order.

e.p: I heard that many online shops now offer free returns. Is that important for you when you buy clothes online?
e.m: Cho biết việc trả hàng miễn phí có quan trọng với bạn không và giải thích lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'Yes, it's very important...' và 'because'.
e.s: Yes, it's very important, especially for clothes. I need to try them on, and sometimes the size is not right, so I need to return them easily.

e.p: You bought a product that had great online reviews, but it was much smaller than you expected when it arrived. What did you do, and what did you learn?
e.m: Kể lại cách bạn xử lý tình huống đó và một bài học bạn đã rút ra.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể sự kiện và thì hiện tại để nói bài học.
e.s: I returned it to the store. I learned that I should always check the product dimensions carefully online, because pictures can be deceiving.

e.p: My friend buys almost everything online, but she often complains about slow shipping and sometimes gets the wrong item. What advice would you give her about checking reviews for shipping and accuracy before ordering?
e.m: Đưa ra hai lời khuyên cho bạn về việc kiểm tra đánh giá về giao hàng và độ chính xác của sản phẩm trước khi đặt hàng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'She should always check...' và 'Also, she needs to...'.
e.s: She should always check the delivery reviews before buying, to see if it is fast. Also, she needs to read product descriptions and reviews carefully for accuracy, because some items might look different in pictures.

d.t: Phân tích xu hướng trong đánh giá sản phẩm

e.p: I see a lot of reviews for this new game, and most of them say it's super fun. What is the general trend in the reviews?
e.m: Nêu xu hướng chung của các đánh giá về trò chơi.
e.h: Dùng 'The general trend is...' hoặc 'Most people think...'.
e.s: The general trend is very positive. Most people think it's a very fun game, and they enjoy playing it.

e.p: This old car model suddenly has many new negative reviews about engine problems. What do you think is happening?
e.m: Đưa ra giả thuyết về lý do xuất hiện nhiều đánh giá tiêu cực mới.
e.h: Dùng 'Perhaps...' hoặc 'It could be because...'.
e.s: Perhaps the cars are getting older, so they start to have problems. It could be because the engine parts are wearing out.

e.p: A popular tourist attraction started getting mixed reviews – some people love it, some hate it. What could be the reasons for this change in feedback?
e.m: Đề xuất hai lý do có thể dẫn đến đánh giá trái chiều về điểm du lịch.
e.h: Dùng 'Maybe they changed...' hoặc 'It's also possible that...'.
e.s: Maybe they changed something, like the entry price or some attractions. It's also possible that expectations are different now, so some visitors feel disappointed.

e.p: I've been looking at reviews for a famous brand of sportswear. For their new collection, many reviews say the quality is not as good as before, even though the designs are nice. How might this trend affect their sales in the future, and what should the company do?
e.m: Phân tích ảnh hưởng của xu hướng này đến doanh số và đề xuất một hành động công ty nên làm.
e.h: Sử dụng 'This trend might...' và 'The company should...'.
e.s: This trend might lower their sales in the future because people care about quality. The company should listen to customer feedback and improve their product quality, so customers will be happier.

e.p: A new online course just launched. In the first week, reviews were all 5 stars, but now, after a month, more 3-star and 4-star reviews are appearing, mentioning difficulty with certain modules. What does this trend tell us about the course and its target audience, and what action should the course creator take?
e.m: Phân tích xu hướng đánh giá, chỉ ra vấn đề về độ khó và nhóm đối tượng, và đề xuất một hành động cho người tạo khóa học.
e.h: Dùng 'This trend suggests...' và 'The creator should...'.
e.s: This trend suggests the course might be too difficult for some students after the basics. The creator should offer more support for those modules or make them clearer, so more students can succeed and give higher ratings.

d.t: Ảnh hưởng của đánh giá đến uy tín thương hiệu

e.p: This new restaurant has received great reviews and is now very popular. How do good reviews help a business?
e.m: Nêu một lợi ích chính của đánh giá tốt đối với doanh nghiệp.
e.h: Dùng 'Good reviews help...' và 'because'.
e.s: Good reviews help a business get more customers because people trust what others say. They also make the restaurant look better.

e.p: A small hotel just got some very bad reviews online. How will this affect its reputation?
e.m: Mô tả một tác động tiêu cực của đánh giá xấu đến danh tiếng khách sạn.
e.h: Dùng 'Bad reviews can...' và 'so'.
e.s: Bad reviews can hurt its reputation a lot. Fewer people will want to stay there, so the hotel might lose business.

e.p: My friend runs a small shop, and she wants to get more positive reviews. What advice should I give her to improve her shop's online image?
e.m: Đề xuất hai cách để cửa hàng nhỏ cải thiện hình ảnh trực tuyến thông qua đánh giá.
e.h: Sử dụng 'She should encourage...' và 'Also, she needs to...'.
e.s: She should encourage happy customers to write reviews. Also, she needs to respond to all feedback, both good and bad, because that shows she cares.

e.p: A big clothing brand recently had a scandal, and many negative reviews appeared online, talking about poor quality. How might this situation impact their brand loyalty and future sales, and what strategy should they adopt to rebuild trust?
e.m: Phân tích tác động đến lòng trung thành và doanh số, và đề xuất một chiến lược để xây dựng lại niềm tin.
e.h: Dùng 'This situation might...' và 'They must...'.
e.s: This situation might make customers lose trust and buy less from them. They must improve quality and communicate openly with customers, so people will believe in them again.

e.p: A local bookstore has always focused on personal customer service but rarely asks for online reviews. A new chain bookstore opened nearby and uses aggressive online marketing and review campaigns. How will this affect the local store's online presence and overall brand strength, and what should the local store do to compete effectively?
e.m: Phân tích tác động đến sự hiện diện trực tuyến và sức mạnh thương hiệu của cửa hàng địa phương, và đề xuất hai hành động họ nên làm để cạnh tranh.
e.h: Dùng 'The local store might...' và 'They should start...' và 'Also, they can...'.
e.s: The local store might lose customers because people search online. They should start asking loyal customers for reviews online. Also, they can highlight their unique personal service, because this is something the chain stores cannot easily offer.

d.t: Sử dụng đánh giá để cải thiện sản phẩm/dịch vụ

e.p: We got a lot of customer feedback saying our new coffee tasted too bitter. What should we do?
e.m: Đề xuất một hành động để cải thiện chất lượng cà phê.
e.h: Sử dụng 'We should...' hoặc 'It's a good idea to...'.
e.s: We should change the coffee beans or try a different brewing method. It's a good idea to listen to customer feedback.

e.p: Many reviews for our online course say that the quizzes are too difficult. How can we make them better?
e.m: Đề xuất hai cách để cải thiện độ khó của bài kiểm tra.
e.h: Dùng 'We could make...' và 'Also, we should...'.
e.s: We could make the quizzes simpler, or give more examples in the lessons. Also, we should explain difficult parts more clearly.

e.p: Our hotel's breakfast received several negative reviews about the limited options. What changes should we make to the breakfast menu?
e.m: Đề xuất hai thay đổi cụ thể để cải thiện thực đơn bữa sáng, nêu hai loại món ăn.
e.h: Dùng 'We should add...' và 'Also, we could...'.
e.s: We should add more fruit and healthier options to the breakfast. Also, we could offer more local dishes, so guests have more choices.

e.p: The new update for our mobile app received mixed reviews; many users love the new features, but some complain about frequent crashes. What steps should our development team take to address these issues and improve user satisfaction?
e.m: Đề xuất hai bước nhóm phát triển nên thực hiện để giải quyết vấn đề và cải thiện trải nghiệm người dùng.
e.h: Dùng 'They should first...' và 'Then, they need to...'.
e.s: They should first find and fix the bugs causing the crashes quickly. Then, they need to test the app more carefully before the next update, so users will have a stable app.

e.p: Our restaurant noticed a trend in reviews: many customers say the waiting time for food is too long, especially on weekends. This is making them disappointed. What concrete actions should the restaurant manager take to reduce waiting times and improve customer experience, even when busy?
e.m: Đề xuất hai hành động cụ thể cho quản lý nhà hàng để giảm thời gian chờ và cải thiện trải nghiệm khách hàng vào cuối tuần.
e.h: Sử dụng 'The manager should...' và 'Also, they can...'.
e.s: The manager should hire more kitchen staff for weekends to prepare food faster. Also, they can offer free small appetizers to customers who wait a long time, so they feel happier and less disappointed.

d.t: Ôn tập: Thảo luận về mua sắm thông minh

e.p: I want to buy a new laptop, but I don't know much about computers. What's the first thing I should do?
e.m: Khuyên người bạn nên tìm kiếm thông tin và đọc đánh giá trước khi mua.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should...' và 'Then, you can...'.
e.s: You should first research different models online. Then, you can read customer reviews to see what other people think.

e.p: I found a great deal online, but there are no reviews for the product. Is it still a good idea to buy it?
e.m: Cho lời khuyên không nên mua sản phẩm không có đánh giá, nêu lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'No, you shouldn't...' và 'because'.
e.s: No, you shouldn't buy it without reviews. It might be a scam or a bad product, because you don't know its quality.

e.p: My old washing machine broke, and I need a new one urgently. I found two models: Model A is cheaper but has mixed reviews, while Model B is more expensive but has excellent reviews for reliability. Which one should I choose?
e.m: So sánh hai mẫu máy giặt và khuyên chọn mẫu tốt hơn cho nhu cầu cấp bách và đáng tin cậy.
e.h: Dùng cấu trúc so sánh hơn và 'so'.
e.s: You should choose Model B. It's more expensive, but reliability is very important for a washing machine, so you will save money in the long run.

e.p: I want to buy a new smartphone, and I've narrowed it down to two models. One has amazing camera reviews, but some users complain about short battery life. The other has an average camera but excellent battery life. I often travel. Which one should I buy, and why?
e.m: Khuyên chọn điện thoại phù hợp với nhu cầu du lịch, giải thích hai lý do.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should choose...' và 'because'.
e.s: You should choose the phone with excellent battery life. When you travel, you need your phone to last all day, because you might not find a charger easily. The camera is good, but battery is more important for travel.

e.p: My sister wants to open a small online shop selling handmade jewelry. She knows that online reviews are important. What two crucial pieces of advice should I give her about managing customer reviews from day one to build a strong reputation?
e.m: Đưa ra hai lời khuyên quan trọng cho người bạn về việc quản lý đánh giá để xây dựng danh tiếng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'She should always...' và 'Also, it's important to...'.
e.s: She should always ask happy customers to leave a review after buying, because good feedback helps her shop. Also, it's important to respond quickly to all reviews, both positive and negative, so customers feel heard and valued.

d.t: Ôn tập: Kể về một lần mua hàng thất vọng

e.p: Have you ever bought something online that was disappointing? What was it?
e.m: Kể tên món đồ bạn mua bị thất vọng.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại.
e.s: Yes, I bought a pair of shoes online. They were very disappointing.

e.p: You said you bought some disappointing shoes. What was the problem with them?
e.m: Mô tả hai vấn đề chính của đôi giày đó.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để diễn tả.
e.s: The size was wrong, and the quality was very bad. They looked different from the picture, too.

e.p: Oh no, that sounds bad. What did you do after you received the wrong size and bad quality shoes?
e.m: Kể lại cách bạn xử lý tình huống đó và kết quả.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để mô tả hành động.
e.s: I contacted the seller, and I sent the shoes back. They gave me a refund, so it was okay in the end.

e.p: I read a review where a customer bought an expensive electronic device, but it stopped working after a month. The customer was very angry. What do you think the company should have done to prevent such bad experiences, and what is the best way for the customer to get help?
e.m: Đề xuất một hành động công ty nên làm để phòng ngừa và một cách tốt nhất để khách hàng nhận được trợ giúp.
e.h: Sử dụng 'The company should have...' và 'The customer should...'.
e.s: The company should have better quality control before selling the device. The customer should contact customer service with their receipt and ask for a new product or a refund.

e.p: You once bought a smartphone that had many positive reviews for its camera, but you found it terrible after using it. How did your personal experience differ from the online reviews, and what did you learn about trusting reviews completely?
e.m: Mô tả sự khác biệt giữa trải nghiệm của bạn và đánh giá trực tuyến, và một bài học bạn đã rút ra về việc tin vào đánh giá.
e.h: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại và thì hiện tại để nói bài học.
e.s: My camera took blurry pictures, but reviews said it was great. I learned that I should not always trust reviews completely, because personal experience can be very different. Now I also try to test products if possible.

d.t: Ôn tập: Lập kế hoạch mua sắm tiếp theo

e.p: I'm going to buy a new laptop next month. What kind of research should I do before going to the store?
e.m: Đề xuất một hành động bạn nên làm để chuẩn bị trước khi mua hàng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should...' và 'so you can...'.
e.s: You should check prices online and read many reviews, so you can compare different models.

e.p: I want to buy a new pair of running shoes. What specific features should I look for in product descriptions and reviews?
e.m: Nêu hai tính năng quan trọng cần tìm kiếm trong mô tả sản phẩm và đánh giá.
e.h: Dùng 'You should look for...' và 'Also, check for...'.
e.s: You should look for comfort and good support for your feet. Also, check for durability in reviews, so they last long.

e.p: I need a new washing machine, and I found one with good reviews for washing clothes well, but some people said it's very noisy. Should I still consider it, or look for a quieter one?
e.m: Khuyên nên xem xét mức độ quan trọng của tiếng ồn đối với bạn và gợi ý hành động khác.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should think about...' và 'Perhaps you can...'.
e.s: You should think about how much noise bothers you at home. Perhaps you can find another model that is quieter, even if it costs a little more.

e.p: My family is planning a beach vacation. I need to book a hotel that is family-friendly and close to the beach, and has positive reviews for cleanliness. What are the three most important things I should check in hotel reviews before booking, to ensure a great experience?
e.m: Nêu ba điều quan trọng nhất bạn nên kiểm tra trong đánh giá khách sạn cho kỳ nghỉ gia đình ở bãi biển.
e.h: Sử dụng 'You should check for...' và 'Also, look at...' và 'Finally, make sure...'.
e.s: You should check for family-friendly facilities like pools or play areas. Also, look at reviews about cleanliness, especially in the rooms. Finally, make sure it is close to the beach, because that is important for a beach vacation.

e.p: I'm going to buy a new gaming console, and I've read many reviews for two popular models. Model X is known for its exclusive games and has amazing graphics, but it's expensive. Model Y is cheaper, has a wider variety of games, but graphics are not as good. I want a good gaming experience, but I also care about value. What would be your final recommendation for me, considering both reviews and my priorities?
e.m: Đưa ra lời khuyên cuối cùng, cân nhắc giữa đồ họa, giá cả, và sự đa dạng game, và giải thích lựa chọn của bạn dựa trên ưu tiên của người dùng.
e.h: Sử dụng 'If you prioritize...' và 'but if you want...'.
e.s: If you prioritize the best graphics and exclusive games, then Model X is better. But if you want a good gaming experience with more game options and a better price, then Model Y is a smarter choice, so you save money and have many games to play.
